swe-architect-mcp.//description: Cross-platform template for running SWE Architect MCP as a local stdio MCP server. Replace placeholders before use.
swe-architect-mcp.//command_note: Use the Python executable where this package is installed. Windows examples: 'python' or 'C:\path\to\swe-architect-mcp\.venv\Scripts\python.exe'. macOS/Linux examples: 'python3', '/path/to/swe-architect-mcp/.venv/bin/python', or './.venv/bin/python'.
swe-architect-mcp.//cwd_note: Use an absolute path when possible. If running from source, set this to the swe-architect-mcp repo path. If the package is installed globally/in a venv, this can be the workspace where lifecycle artifacts should be created. On Windows, escape backslashes like 'C:\Users\you\project' or use forward slashes.
swe-architect-mcp.//uv_note: If you prefer uv, set command to 'uv' and args to ['run', '--directory', '<ABSOLUTE_PATH_TO_REPO>', 'python', '-m', 'swe_architect_mcp'].
swe-architect-mcp.//env_note: Use either SE_MCP_API_KEY + SE_MCP_MODEL, or provider-specific keys such as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, or GOOGLE_API_KEY. Do not commit real keys.
